raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX, I purchased a XXXX item for {$260.00} from XXXX XXXX - XXXX XXXX XXXX at the XXXX XXXX. 

After returning to New York and closely inspecting the item, I discovered material inconsistencies and initiated authentication. The item was determined to be counterfeit by a XXXX professional authentication service. 

Despite providing expert documentation, American Express has refused to conduct a meaningful investigation and has repeatedly closed my dispute. 

Timeline of Events below : - XX/XX/XXXX : My initial dispute was closed after the merchant submitted generic franchisee/authorization certificates. No authentication of the actual item was performed. 

- XX/XX/XXXX : I reopened the dispute and provided additional contextual evidence. 

- XX/XX/XXXX : American Express closed the reopened dispute and instructed me in writing to provide an expert report confirming the item was counterfeit. 

XX/XX/XXXX : In reliance on American Express written instructions, I obtained and paid for a professional XXXX authentication report from Real Authentication ( Report XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX ). The report formally determined the item is Counterfeit. I uploaded this documentation immediately. 

XX/XX/XXXX : American Express closed the dispute again, and instructed me in writing to provide an expert report confirming the item was counterfeit again. 

Upon calling American Express immediately after the third closure, I was told explicitly by a representative that : - The case would not be reopened. 
- The authentication report would not be considered. 
- The decision had already been made in favor of the merchant. 
- I should instead contact AMEX XXXX XXXX, which covers theft or accidental damage not counterfeit goods. 

This response is inappropriate. This is not a theft or damage claim. This is a dispute involving counterfeit merchandise and failure to receive goods as described. 

Basis of Complaint American Express : - Required an expert report. 
- I obtained and submitted that report at my own expense. 
- The report conclusively determined the item is counterfeit. 
- The bank then refused to consider the report and declined to reopen the case. 

The merchants submission of general franchise documentation does not authenticate the specific item I received. American Express has chosen to rely on generic merchant status documents rather than direct, item-specific counterfeit evidence. 

This reflects a failure to conduct a reasonable investigation under consumer protection standards governing billing disputes.
